sections.content: The player has moved right and is now in the right S corridor. I can see the player is now near the right side. The level layout has changed - I can see green blocks (N) in different positions now. Let me check the current player position and the level state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and level state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Player: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Yellow: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if any N blocks changed
n_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"\nN blocks: {len(n_blocks)}")
for n in n_blocks[:5]:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Check score/state
print(f"\nLast action result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
ACTION7 didn't work - it says "Unknown action at index 1". Maybe the action name is different. Let me try MOUSE instead. Looking at the image, there are green blocks that might be clickable targets. Let me try clicking on one of them with MOUSE.
First, let me figure out the coordinates of the green blocks from the current frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check N block positions for MOUSE targeting
seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks count: {len(n_blocks)}")
for n in n_blocks:
    # Get center of each block
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    center_r = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    center_c = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    print(f"  N block: center=({center_r}, {center_c}), boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Try clicking on the first N block
if n_blocks:
    n0 = n_blocks[0]
    rows = [p[0] for p in n0['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n0['boundary']]
    target_r = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    target_c = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    print(f"\nClicking on N block at ({target_r}, {target_c})")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': target_r, 'col': target_c}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
